Experts have warned that petrol could hit £2 a litre for the first time, amid a staggering spike in global oil costs. Keir Starmer is desperately trying to calm fears of another 2022-style cost of living squeeze, hinting at another bailout despite the fragile state of the government's finances. He is also scrambling to limit damage to the Special Relationship from his refusal to back Donald Trump's decision to launch the war on Iran. The president has dismissed the soaring oil and gas prices saying they are a 'small price to pay' for taming Tehran. The price of a barrel of oil has rocketed over $100 for the first time in years, with supplies threatened by the closure of the Strait of Hormuz attacks on infrastructure of major producers in the region.

I’m in my 70’s and had this problem from a tiny child. I pulled out both my eyelashes and hair My older brother use to constantly hit and bully me. He got sent away to boarding school and my parents bought me a pony. I literally stopped overnight. It was obviously a stress reliever, I can still remember how good it felt to do it. These days my issues would’ve been picked up early on, I felt ashamed and embarrassed when other children would ask why my eyes were funny and I had bald patches on my head. Years later my mother apologised for not recognising how his bullying had affected me.

Aged six, I had beautiful, long, blonde hair. Mum would spend hours lovingly brushing it. Yet, by the time I was ten, I had pulled it all out.

EXCLUSIVE: Lucy Hargreaves was asleep on her living room sofa when three men burst in and blasted her with a sawn-off shotgun. Ignoring the cries of her two-year-old daughter, they then doused her home, in Lambourne Avenue in the Liverpool suburb of Walton, with petrol and set it alight, forcing her partner Gary Campbell to escape through an upstairs window with the toddler in his arms. After two men put on trial for murder had the charges dropped due to insufficient evidence, police efforts are now focused on tracking down the fugitive prime suspect, Kevin Parle. As the hunt for Parle continues more than two decades on, new information has emerged about the 45-year-old's former gangland associations in Liverpool.

Watch this... the Bank of England have been failing to reduce inflation back to its 2% target. It's been using rate cuts (inflationary) to bailout Labour's lack of a plan for growth instead of reserving then for genuine economic emergencies. Labour has failed on inflation by introducing inflationary policy. They have also raised more stealth tax via fiscal drag/ the freezing of tax thresholds. Now both will ignore all that and point the finger at a war that neither has publicly condemned and now Labour is proactively getting the UK involved in. It's the excuse they were waiting for and the an opportunity to raise even more tax through fiscal drag as the tax threshold freezes have been extended. It's the people that will continue to pay the price, particularly as the UK has the highest energy costs thanks to the uni-party's decades of failure on energy policy.

It’s the Isle of Man with the Max cat ! This article confuses 2 islands

Skip the 26-hour, 10,000-mile journey to the Southern Hemisphere - Tasmania's twin is within easy reach just off the British coast. Cited as the 'Tasmania of the British Isles,' the Isle of Man captures that same wild, fresh island spirit without the jet lag. There, rugged cliffs, pristine beaches, and vibrant grasslands similar to that of the Australian island await, but are reachable in under an hour. Whether it's the unique wildlife or the fresh island energy you're after, you can find total coastal magic with one quick trip on the ferry.
  • 61 comments
  • share Inside the 'Tasmania of the British Isles' with barely-touched beaches
